steps.title: Read the intercepts
steps.content: From the graph, the line crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,6)$ and the $x$-axis at $(2,0)$. Put those two points in Desmos as a table instead of doing a long symbolic setup.

steps.title: Fit the line and clear decimals
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. The regression gives $m=-3$ and $b=6$, so the graph is $y=-3x+6$. Rewriting gives $3x+y=6$, and multiplying by $6$ gives $18x+6y=36$. That matches the form $18x+Ry=36$ with positive $R=6$.

steps.title: Rule out the other forms
steps.content: Choices A and B put the fixed coefficient $18$ on the $y$-term, so at $(0,6)$ they force $18(6)=36$, which is false. Choice D has the wrong sign: at the $x$-intercept it gives $18(2)-R(0)=-36$, or $36=-36$. Only choice C matches both intercepts.
